script:
Up.
Shorts on.
Water bottle in hand.

David Goggins ran his first hundred-mile race on two broken feet and stress fractures he didn't know about until mile seventy. He didn't have a crowd. He didn't have a reason anyone else could see. He had a decision he made alone, in the dark, before the world was watching.

That's the only kind that counts.

You said it last night:
do the morning lift before opening any work doc.

The doc can wait.
The inbox can wait.
The lift cannot.

no one is coming, it's on you.

Shorts are on.
Feet hit the floor.
Walk to the gym.

Not the idea of it.
The actual door.
The actual floor under your feet.
Right now.

mode: gym_now

user's hero (reference by concrete habit, never by invented quote): David Goggins
user's grounding phrase (use verbatim, do not paraphrase): "no one is coming, it's on you"
